items.title: OpenAI's GPT 5.6 is the 'preferred model' for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
items.summary: OpenAI confirms GPT 5.6 as the favored model for Microsoft Copilot 365, amidst ongoing speculation about potential tensions or a 'breakup' between the two AI giants.

items.title: OpenCoF: Learning to Reason Through Video Generation
items.summary: Researchers introduce OpenCoF, a new framework that enables AI models to learn reasoning capabilities by generating videos, marking a significant step towards more intelligent video understanding.

items.title: An AI agent startup just let its agent run its $100M fundraise
items.summary: A new AI agent startup successfully closed a $100 million funding round, notably allowing its own AI agent to autonomously manage the entire fundraising process.

items.title: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing
items.summary: OpenAI is discontinuing its Atlas project, yet simultaneously signals continued and expanding ambitions in the development of AI-powered browser technologies.

items.title: Nous Research's NousCoder-14B is an open-source coding model landing right in the Claude Code moment
items.summary: Nous Research has released NousCoder-14B, a new open-source coding model that positions itself as a strong competitor in the rapidly evolving landscape of AI-powered code generation, particularly against models like Claude Code.

items.title: CineMobile: On-Device Image-to-Video Diffusion for Cinematic Camera Motion Generation
items.summary: CineMobile introduces an on-device image-to-video diffusion model capable of generating cinematic camera motions, bringing advanced video creation to mobile platforms.

items.title: Simple Benchmark Review: Ollama on Jetson Nano
items.summary: A practical benchmark review evaluates the performance of Ollama, a tool for running large language models locally, on the resource-constrained Jetson Nano device.

items.title: Linear Attention Architectures: Mechanisms, Trade-offs, and Cross-Layer Routing
items.summary: This paper delves into the intricacies of linear attention architectures, analyzing their mechanisms, performance trade-offs, and novel cross-layer routing strategies for improved efficiency.

items.title: RoboDojo: A Unified Sim-and-Real Benchmark for Comprehensive Evaluation of Generalist Robot Manipulation Policies
items.summary: RoboDojo presents a new unified benchmark that bridges simulation and real-world environments to thoroughly evaluate generalist robot manipulation policies, advancing the field of robotics.

items.title: Let an AI clear out your false positives without letting it hide a real bug
items.summary: This article explores strategies for leveraging AI to filter out false positives in bug detection while ensuring that genuine software defects are not inadvertently concealed.

items.title: Remember When It Matters: Proactive Memory Agent for Long-Horizon Agents
items.summary: Researchers introduce a proactive memory agent designed to enhance the performance of long-horizon AI agents by selectively recalling relevant information when needed.

items.title: Sparse Delta Memory: Scaling the State of Linear RNNs through Sparsity
items.summary: This paper proposes Sparse Delta Memory, a method that leverages sparsity to efficiently scale the state of linear Recurrent Neural Networks, improving their capacity and performance.

items.title: Automating the Design of Embodied Agent Architectures
items.summary: This research focuses on automating the design process for embodied agent architectures, aiming to streamline the development of intelligent systems that interact with physical environments.

items.title: InsightsTrack + Pulse: I taught Claude Desktop to read my web analytics (via MCP)
items.summary: A developer successfully integrated Claude Desktop with web analytics tools (InsightsTrack + Pulse) using an MCP (Multi-modal Control Protocol) to enable the AI to interpret and act on data.

items.title: Scalable Visual Pretraining for Language Intelligence
items.summary: This paper explores scalable visual pretraining techniques designed to significantly enhance the language intelligence capabilities of AI models.
